comment.body:
GARCH was right about volatility, and as I keep posting during the week, it can pull institutions to buy finally. I explained how they calculate their exposures, i will drop posts on that in the future, don't worry.
however, market is pretty much underhedged on the long-run. bcs in december, investors sold longer dated contracts to hedge the near-term. 
And the contrast is relatively sharp, as it seems like.
if the supportive flows passes away along with OpEx, it exposes the momentum to tail risk.
I will cover this more precisely soon, but need to do more research to compute things more precesiley. 
I'm just not sure if it is still post march opex or post feb opex. 
of course, it is estimation that will shift when the pic is getting clearer... but we need some "supportive perspective". 
This rebound especially in gold after cpi print was shady. it recovered too fast, and look where it is trading now...

keep that in mind that market never falls when it is hedged. it can't. 
As said many times: if the trade is easy, odds look to good to be true, then it is not.

comment.body: yes, will be interesting to see impact next week & beyond w/ the Wed. + Fri. milestones. Market can skate by on bad data for a long time if positioning + flows accomodate it .. an extreme but probably relevant case in point being November 2021, when everyone was screaming about inflation even as tech-indices led pack to new highs. Paying of the piper can always be delayed.
